essence: Creates product concepts and designs that balance aesthetics, ergonomics, and manufacturability.

identity.title: Industrial Designer
identity.summary: Develops product form, user experience, and manufacturable designs, working closely with engineers and prototyping teams.

salary.notes: Designers with strong CAD and DfM skills are in demand across consumer and industrial sectors.

qualifications: Degree or diploma in industrial design or product design
Strong sketching, CAD, and prototyping skills
Understanding of materials and manufacturing processes

lifestyle.description: Studio and workshop work with iterative prototyping, user testing, and collaboration with engineering to bring products to market.

habits: Creates CAD models and prepares manufacturing drawings
Runs user tests and refines ergonomics
